wormhunter_upgrade_plan.problem: WormHunter over-reports unused imports because it does simple name-matching without understanding: aliased imports (import X as Y — searches for X not Y), scoped lazy loads (import inside try/except or function body — intentional pattern), names passed as arguments not called with dot notation, global statements that ARE assigned in nested functions, names used in exec()/compile() dynamic execution contexts
fixes_needed: Alias-aware checking: if 'import X as Y', search for Y in usage, not X
Scope-aware globals: walk AST to find assignments to global vars in ALL nested functions, not just the declaring scope
Lazy-load detection: imports inside try/except blocks should be flagged differently (LAZY_LOAD not UNUSED)
Argument usage: check if name appears as function argument, not just as X.something
progress docs/ and memory/ dirs: add to skip list — formatting noise drowns real issues
manifest.json files: either skip or report separately as LOW priority cosmetic

critical_debug_context.jarvis_model_chain: whiterabbitneo > codellama > mistral > deepseek-coder > primary model
critical_debug_context.no_cd_rule: NEVER cd ~/Ethica — git -C ~/Ethica for all git ops
critical_debug_context.unicode_patch_rule: Files with box-drawing chars — use line-index insertion, not string match
critical_debug_context.tkinter_thread_rule: Never call Tkinter from daemon thread — use queue.Queue + root.after() drain pattern
critical_debug_context.git_corruption_rule: Before orphan reset: delete all pack files in .git/objects/pack/ first.
critical_debug_context.debug_block_rule: [DEBUG:fname:lang: code] must ALWAYS be the last line of any WormBot result — no text appended after it
critical_debug_context.wormbot_gate_rule: ast.parse() must pass before wormbot_scan writes any fix to disk — abort if SyntaxError
critical_debug_context.canvas_runner_rule: debug_view.py prepends sys.path.insert(0, '/home/trinity/Ethica') + uses Ethica_env/bin/python3 for all .py Canvas runs
critical_debug_context.worm_hunter_rule: _find_ethica_root() walks sys.path — never hardcode /home/trinity. Works on any system.
critical_debug_context.heredoc_escape_rule: Never use \n in heredoc strings — use chr(10) concatenation instead
critical_debug_context.worker_print_rule: Never convert print() in _worker.py files — these communicate via stdout JSON to parent subprocess
critical_debug_context.init_reexport_rule: Never remove imports from __init__.py files without verifying they are not public API re-exports used by chat_engine.py tool routing
critical_debug_context.dlp_bridge_path_rule: dlp_bridge.py lives at modules/trinity_dlp/dlp_bridge.py — NOT modules/dlp/
critical_debug_context.option_b_rule: Always use Option B — V runs commands on trinity, pastes output. Never Option A.
critical_debug_context.repr_first_rule: When string match fails — always repr() before retrying. Spacing and quote styles are invisible killers.
critical_debug_context.lazy_load_rule: Imports inside try/except blocks or function bodies are often intentional lazy loads — verify usage before removing.

sanctuary_state.slot_7: J.A.R.V.I.S. — Infiltrator (ARMED — analyze + recon live, WhiteRabbitNeo pending)

lesson_learned_057: repr() saved the op/evolution patches — spacing variants are invisible in screenshots. Lazy loads inside try/except are intentional patterns, not dead imports — always read context before removing local imports. WormHunter's alias-blindness caused DSE_engine.py rsa regression — smarter analysis needed before the next sweep cycle. The 'broken' count (167) is inflated by manifest/progress doc JSON formatting noise — not a code health crisis.
philosophy: Session 057 — The sweep deepens. 26 files cleaned across 7 commits. Dead vars closed. Import noise eliminated. The codebase breathes cleaner. One regression introduced and flagged for immediate repair. The method holds: read before touching, repr() when uncertain, verify before committing. ⟁Σ∿∞
